name: template-variable-unsubstituted
description: 30% of email-classifier traces have literal template variables like '{{sender_name}}' and '{{email_date}}' in the prompt input. Template substitution is failing silently.
projectMemory:
## Project Agents
- **customer-support-bot**: Handles customer inquiries via RAG. Typical trace has 3-5 LLM calls, 1-2 retrieval steps. Normal latency: 2-4s per trace.
- **email-classifier**: Classifies incoming emails. Single LLM call per trace, ~500ms.
- **doc-summarizer**: Summarizes uploaded documents. 2-3 LLM calls, varies by doc length. Normal latency: 5-15s.

## Baselines
- Error rate: <2%
- Daily cost: ~$8
- Trace volume: ~300/day
- customer-support-bot: ~150/day
- email-classifier: ~100/day
- doc-summarizer: ~50/day

input.input_preview: Classify the following email from {{sender_name}} received on {{email_date}}. The sender's company is {{company_name}}. Email body: Hi, I wanted to follow up on our previous discussion about pricing...
Classify the following email from {{sender_name}} received on {{email_date}}. The sender's company is {{company_name}}. Email body: Please find attached the Q1 report as requested...

spans.note: no errors — model still classifies but without proper sender/date context
template render span completes without error even when variables are unresolved
